weeks.content:
Race week. Short shake-outs only. The fitness is locked in — nothing you do this week adds to it, but poor choices can take away from it. Sleep, eat, stay off your feet. Race day is Saturday, August 15.

**Sauna stack:** Heat protocol is active. Post-run 20-30 min in 175-195°F sauna, daily through race day Aug 14. Plasma volume gains hold 7-14 days. **Hot car alternative**: park in sun, windows up, sit 20-30 min, hydrate going in, exit if HR > 140 or dizzy. **Hot bath alternative**: 104°F for 30-40 min.

**RACE DAY: Saturday Aug 15, 5:30 AM start, 9:00 PM cutoff (15.5 hrs)**

Cut-off pace targets per segment (avg 18:36/mi overall):
- Start → AS2 Surveyors (mi 18.9): 11:15 AM cutoff. **Target: arrive by 10:45 AM** for 30-min buffer (16:40/mi)
- AS2 → AS3 High Prairie (mi 26.5): 1:45 PM cutoff (19:44/mi for the climb)
- AS3 → AS4 Bennett Pass (mi 35.8): 4:30 PM (17:44/mi, technical descent + ridgeline)
- AS4 → AS5 Umbrella (mi 40.1): 5:45 PM (17:26/mi)
- AS5 → AS6 Ski Patrol (mi 42.6): 6:30 PM (18:00/mi, steep service road)
- AS6 → Finish (mi 50.0): 9:00 PM (20:16/mi for the crux + descent)

**Crux**: AS6 → AS7 = 1.6 mi at ~18% grade gaining 1,500 ft to 8,000 ft. Power-hike with consistent cadence. This is the BPA you've been training, at altitude.

**Carb load (Aug 11-14):**
- Mon-Tue: normal eating, 50% carbs, drop alcohol
- Wed: last harder shake-out, normal carbs
- Thu (Aug 13): start carb load. 5-7g carbs/kg. Reduce fiber, fat. White rice, pasta, white bread, bananas, jam, honey, dates
- Fri (Aug 14): same carb load. Bib pickup 5-6:30 PM. Dinner by 6:30 PM (low-fiber pasta + small protein, white bread, banana). Hydrate with electrolytes. Bed by 8:30 PM

**Race breakfast (Aug 15, alarm 2:30 AM):**
- 2:45-3:00 AM: 80-120g carbs, low fiber, low fat
  - Option: 1.5 cups white rice + 2 tbsp honey + 1 banana + pinch salt
  - Option: 2 slices white toast + 2 tbsp jam + 1 banana
- 16-20 oz water with electrolytes
- Sip electrolytes on shuttle (3:30-5:00 AM)
- 5:00 AM: 1 gel + sip water, don't over-hydrate

**During race fueling:**
- Gels every 30-40 min from start (your homemade)
- Precision 750mg/hr flask: refill at AS2 (mi 18.9) and AS5 (mi 40.1). Carry 2 flasks if you can
- S-caps backup if flask runs dry
- Caffeinated gels: AS3 (mi 26.5), AS5 (mi 40.1), AS6 (mi 42.6) — ~300mg total over second half
- Aid food: bananas, PB&J halves, Neversecond C30 backup if homemade runs out

**Drop bag plan:**
- AS2 (mi 18.9): backup gels, full Precision refill mix, sunscreen, anti-chafe, spare s-caps
- AS5 (mi 40.1): **headlamp + spare batteries** (required if leaving after 5 PM), caffeinated gels, fresh socks, anti-chafe, second flask refill, ibuprofen if you take it

**Other:**
- Pacers NOT allowed
- Trekking poles allowed, gaiters encouraged for AS6-AS7 scree
- Crew: AS1, 2, 5, 6, 7, finish (NOT AS3 or AS4)

days.title: Easy + Hill Strides + PT
days.content:
60 min easy, 6×20 sec hill strides.
- **Distance**: ~4–5 miles

**Wednesday PT — Reactive/athletic + knee — Tier 1**
- Pogo / SL hops: 3×10 each
- Step-downs: 3×15
- Wall sit: 3×45s
- Med ball side-to-side: 3×12

summary.strengthOneLine: Reactive + knee: pogo/SL hops, step-downs, wall sit, med ball

days.title: Race-Specific Climbing
days.content:
**Default: BPA with the group** (.97 mi / 889 ft / 16.6%).
- **Reps**: 2 up + 2 down + .5 up/down (~5 mi / ~2,000 ft)
- **Pace**: power-hike effort, consistent cadence, work toward 30-35 min per up

**If group not going / no drive:**
- Stairways x5 (close to home, power-hike form work) — 4-5 mi / 1,200-1,500 ft
- OR Mt Tabor 7-9% runnable repeats — 5-7 mi / 1,000-1,500 ft

BPA is the most race-specific Thursday option (matches the AS6 → AS7 crux at 18%). Default to it whenever the group is going.

days.title: Long Run
days.content:
1:45–2:00 hours easy.
- **Distance**: ~8–9 miles
- **Vert**: ~1,000 ft
- **Fueling**: Practice 60g carbs/hr

Keep effort easy. Notice how good it feels to run without a race on the horizon.
